videos.transcript: the busy city of Manila the capital of the Philippines is an exciting and energetic Metropolis hop on board any of the colorful and kitchi jeepneys to get around town the iconic buses that usually pack in more people than they can hold are the main form of transportation and worth at least one ride during your visit just for the experience of course Manila has an Eclectic mix of modern attractions and Rich history as evidenced by the many Cathedrals and tributes to the city's 16th century EST establishment alongside modern day malls and amenities some of the best places to visit in Manila are the churches monuments and impeccably landscaped public parks Manila is full of restaurants that serve up local fair including fresh seafood in this video we're going to be taking a look at the top 10 rated tourist attractions and things to do in Manila before we begin make sure to subscribe to our YouTube channel for more awesome travel guides and make sure you hit the notification Bell so that you know when we publish a new video video so now let's cut to the chase at 10 tour Manila on bambike ecotours manila is a pedestrian friendly City but if you want to explore the culture and history more efficiently consider the intramuros bamboo bicycle ecotours these tours provide a unique eco-friendly way to see Urban Manila by taking an Eco tour you are also supporting environmental sustainability efforts of local businesses bamb bikes are locally handmade bicycles custom built from bamboo the company has a social welfare initiative that employs villagers for the production of the bikes at nine Robinson's Place Mall while a mall might not be on the top of your list for exploring the culture of Manila Robinson's Place mall has a surprising number of unique stores where you can find local items while locals shop here it's also a popular spot for tourists Beyond on the lookout for clothing Cosmetics bags and food that are unique to the Philippines the mall is extensive so plan time to walk the interior and explore the stores the mall's modern building is a stark contrast to The Rustic and historic landmarks that surround it next up at eight discover Filipino history at the national museum of the Philippines for a true exploration into the culture of the country the national museum of the Philippines is one of the best places to visit this museum has four main complexes including extensive Collections and artifacts relating to Art archaeology anthropology zoology biology and natural history the museum galleries help offer a sense of place telling the cultural story beyond what you will find on your own visiting the city's many historical attractions and now at 7 view the skyline from Manila Bay strolling along the Manila Bay Waterfront is one of the most enjoyable and Scenic things to do in the city and it does not cost anything to enjoy the natural Harbor is always bustling with activity take a walk along the Manila Bay walk at sunset for a great view of the city skyline the Bayfront is accented by yachts and smaller boats anchored in the harbor and open air cafes where you can stop for a bite to eat the bay front is also a popular place for locals taking in a morning jog another relaxing way to enjoy Manila Bay is to book a sunset dinner cruise that will take you past local landmarks and includes dinner and live entertainment for an evening at 6 admire kapo church and Public Square the kapo church is one of the oldest and most admired Catholic churches in Manila the more formal name name of the church is the minor Basilica of the black Nazarene depending on the time of day The Plaza surrounding the century old church can be quite congested which makes it an interesting place to visit to admire the devotion of the Filipino people Fridays are the busiest days when thousands of people pile into the square and pray at the church for naena despite the large Gatherings of church devotees this is one of the top attractions in the city take time to see the inside of the church and stroll through the market stands outside to buy fresh peanuts mangoes or necklaces from local vendors and now at 5: walk the grounds at Fort Santiago the site of Fort Santiago is an impressive stop if you are visiting the city and it is one of the most important historical sites in Manila this 16th century Fortress was key in protecting the intramuros which is the oldest District in Manila known historically as the war Old City you can explore the grounds at a leisurely Pace be sure to take note of bullet markings that are still visible in the Fortress walls and stop by The Visitor Center for more detailed information about the fort at four stroll through rizel Park one of the first stops in Manila should be rizel Park sometimes referred to as lunetta Park this is a peaceful and manicured outdoor Garden located in the Heart of the City the park is a social Gathering Place in Manila known for its open air concerts and chess Plaza the paved pathways through the park guide you through a lush landscape of blooming flowers ponds and more than 40 points of interest that range from statues to historical dedications of note is the century old Monument of Ral which houses the remains of one of the Philippines most celebrated Heroes Dr Jose Rizal be sure to stroll through the park to learn about the local flora and and forer keep your eyes open for the Nar tree the national tree of the Philippines and the small lake with an artistically raised archipelago structure in the middle at three St Augustine church and Museum as a designated UNESCO world heritage site the San Augustine church is a National Treasure and one of the most popular places to visit in the Philippines this Catholic church has a unique history that is reflected in the details of the building if you look closely you will see both Chinese and Spanish influences in the design the church has been rebuilt several times after battles and natural disasters pay close attention to the carvings on the large wooden doors that open to the chapel if you have an interest in history or architecture it would be worth your time to hire a local guide who can take you on a walking tour of the area and describe intricate details of the church that you may not notice on your own at now at 2 dive deep at Manila Ocean Park the Manila Ocean Park is one of the most unique adventures in the city and one of the top things to do for families visiting Manila get interactive with marine life with the sharks and Rays encounter trails to Antarctica exhibit or the fish spar where fish clean your feet in a small pool of water nice the park easily fills several hours depending on how many encounters you try in addition to the state-of-the-art Aquatic Facility you will see exhibits of birds and other animals the Manila Ocean Park is built as a marine theme park besides the educational aquatic attractions there is shopping dining and Hotel H2O where you can sleep surrounded by an aquarium and finally at 1 experience manila's oldest section in intramuros you will likely pass through the intramuros whether you know it or not as you explore Manila it is the oldest section of the city and characterized by its walled Fortress this section of Manila was once a wall City during Spanish colonial times in the 1500s to the 1800s walk around inside the 3M long stone wall that surrounds the district while you can walk the ground on your own you might want to take a guided walking tour to learn more videos.transcript: Welcome to Manila City, a dynamic capital where rich history, diverse culture, and modern urban life come together in the heart of the Philippines. In this virtual tour, we'll explore some of the top and trending destinations in Manila and discover its fascinating heritage, lively streets, local flavors, and hidden gems waiting to be experienced. Known for being bustling yet full of life, Manila City offers a wide range of activities and places to enjoy. Stay tuned as we cover everything from busy malls and peaceful parks to colorful streets and new attractions to explore. Come along as we uncover the city's lively charm and be your guide for your next city adventure. Welcome to Manila City, Philippines. Number one, take in the views and enjoy a relaxing stroll along the Pasig River Esplanade. Discover the Pasig River Esplanade, a beautiful public walkway where you can unwind and soak in the charm of Manila's riverside for free. Stroll along the scenic path. Capture stunning photos of the city skyline and river views and treat yourself to tasty bites from the food stalls lined along the way. Make your visit extra special by catching the vibrant light show at Jones Bridge every Saturday at 700 p.m. A dazzling display that lights up the night and makes your evening stroll truly memorable. Take your time, enjoy the fresh breeze, and experience this side of Manila that's open to everyone and worth returning to again and again. Number two step back in time and explore the historic Fort Santiago in Intramuros. Now accessible through the Pasig River, Esplanade. Step back in time and explore the historic Fort Santiago in Intramuros, Manila. This iconic fortress stands as one of the most important landmarks in Philippine history. Once serving as a vital military defense during the Spanish colonial era, walk through its centuries old gates. Discover the preserved walls and dungeons and imagine life within these storied grounds. Don't miss the peaceful gardens, old cannons, and the shrine dedicated to national hero José Rzal. Wander freely, capture beautiful moments, and embrace the stories that make Fort Santiago an essential stop in Manila's rich history. Number three wander through time inside Intramuros, Manila. Speaking of Fort Santiago, it's just one part of Intramuros, Manila's famous walled city and the heart of the old capital. Step inside these historic walls, and you'll find cobblestone streets, Spanish colonial buildings, old churches, and charming plazas that bring the past to life. Walk or ride a bike along its storied paths. Visit centuries old museums, and feel the blend of old and new as local cafes and shops breathe new life into this heritage site. Intramuros is a must see for anyone wanting to experience the history, culture, and timeless beauty that make Manila truly special. Number four taste and explore the flavors of Binondo. Manila's iconic food trip destination. Now, let's head to Binondo, Manila's iconic Chinatown and a top destination for food lovers. Known as the world's oldest Chinatown, Binondo is a trending spot for food trips where you can taste everything from authentic dim sum and noodles to local street snacks. Wander through Ongpin Street and its busy side alleys filled with heritage shops, old bakeries, and family run restaurants. It's especially popular during the Chinese New Year season when the streets come alive with colorful decorations, lion dances, and festive treats. If you're looking for good food and rich culture, Binondo is always worth a visit. Number five shop, dine, and unwind at Lucky Chinatown Mall. Just a short walk from the busy streets of Binondo Lucky Chinatown Mall offers a modern twist to your Chinatown experience this vibrant mall blends old traditions with new finds. From delicious Chinese restaurants and local snack stalls to trendy shops and relaxing cafes, it's a favorite spot to cool off after a Binondo food trip or to enjoy seasonal events, especially during Chinese New Year when the mall comes alive with colorful displays and festive performances. Take a break, grab a bite, and enjoy the mix of culture and comfort that makes Lucky Chinatown a go-to hangout in Manila. Number six find great deals and hidden treasures at Divisoria, Manila. Next up is Divisoria, Manila's famous bargain shopping hub where you can find just about anything at affordable prices. Known for its lively streets and endless rows of shops and stalls, Divisoria is the go-to place for budget friendly clothes, fabrics, accessories, party supplies, and so much more. It's especially popular during the holiday season, back to school rush, and whenever people need to buy in bulk for special occasions and celebrations. Dive into the busy alleys, practice your haggling skills, and discover hidden finds that make every visit exciting. Shop smart, hunt for unique items, and enjoy the thrill of scoring amazing deals right in the heart of Manila. Number seven discover culture and street eats in Quiapo, Manila. Now, let's head to Quiapo, one of Manila's most colorful districts known for its deep history, vibrant culture, and lively street life. At the heart of it all is the iconic Quiapo Church, famous for housing the Black Nazarene, a centuries old statue of Jesus Christ that draws millions of devotees every year, especially during the annual Traslacion. Surrounding the church is a maze of bustling streets where a trending food trip scene awaits. From tasty street snacks and local delicacies to hidden stalls serving Filipino favorites. Stroll through the market alleys, hunt for great deals, and savor the food and vibrant atmosphere that make Quiapo a truly memorable part of Manila. Enjoying this virtual tour so far? If you're loving these stops and travel tips, don't forget to hit like and subscribe to see more hidden gems, food trips, and must visit places all around the Philippines. Your support helps us share even more amazing destinations with you. Now, let's keep going and discover what's next. Number eight relax and reflect at Rizal Park, Manila. Next, we visit Rizal Park, also known as Luneta, one of Manila's most beloved and historic public spaces. This iconic park is dedicated to the national hero Dr. Jose Rizal, whose monument stands proudly at the center as a symbol of Filipino freedom and pride. Spread out across wide open lawns, gardens, fountains, and pathways, Rizal Park is a favorite spot for families, friends, and tourists to unwind, have a picnic, or simply enjoy a leisurely stroll. Stay a bit longer and catch the beautiful fountain and light show that brings the park to life at night. Best of all, it's free for everyone to enjoy, especially during the holidays when the whole park lights up with festive decorations and a joyful atmosphere. Number nine explore art and history at the national museums in Rizal Park. While you're at Rizal Park, don't miss the chance to explore the National Museums that surround this historic area. Here you'll find the National Museum of Fine Arts, National Museum of Anthropology, and National Museum of Natural History. All showcasing the Philippines rich culture, heritage, and natural wonders. Wander through grand galleries filled with Filipino masterpieces. Learn about the country's diverse history and traditions and see fascinating exhibits from ancient artifacts to wildlife displays. These museums are open to everyone for free, making them a perfect stop for families, students, and anyone curious about the stories that shape the nation. Number 10 Experience thrilling rides and family fun at Star City Amusement Park. Get ready for a day of excitement at Star City Amusement Park, one of Manila's favorite spots for family fun and thrilling rides. This iconic park has stood the test of time for decades. From adrenaline pumping roller coasters to classic carnival attractions, there's something here for everyone to enjoy. Take a spin on the famous Star Flyer. Play at the arcades, bump around in the bumper cars, or let the kids have a blast on the many family-friendly rides. With colorful lights, fun games, and tasty snacks at every corner, Star City is the perfect place to make fun memories with friends and family. It's especially popular during weekends and holidays, so be sure to plan your visit ahead. Number 11 a convenient city stop at SM Manila. Next on our stop is SM City Manila. An iconic mall right in the heart of the city. Conveniently located beside the central terminal LRT station. This well-loved shopping center is a go-to spot for students, office workers, and families looking for a quick shopping trip, tasty meals, or just a place to relax indoors. From fashion stores and gadget shops to popular restaurants and budget friendly food stalls, SM Manila has plenty to offer for everyone. It's a practical and lively hangout, perfect for taking a break while exploring the busy streets of Manila. Number 12 meet animal friends at the Manila Zoo. Another fun stop in the city is the Manila Zoo. One of the oldest and most well-known zoos in the Philippines. Recently renovated, the zoo now offers a fresher and more family-friendly experience for visitors of all ages. Wander around and see different animals from tigers and birds to reptiles and other wildlife. The spacious grounds also serve as a relaxing hangout spot right in the city with areas where kids can play and learn at the same time. It's a great place to enjoy nature, snap photos, and spend a fun day with family and friends without leaving Manila. Number 13 Dive into fun at Manila Ocean Park Next, we're off to Manila Ocean Park, the city's popular marine themed park and oceanarium. This family-friendly attraction lets you get up close with colorful marine life. From fascinating jellyfish and playful penguins to giant rays and schools of tropical fish don't miss the exciting sea lion and bird shows. Crowd favorites where these amazing animals put on fun performances for everyone to enjoy. With interactive exhibits, walk through tunnels, and souvenir shops, there's plenty to see and do for the whole family. And if you want to stay longer, you can even book a station at Hotel H2O right inside the park for a unique and relaxing experience by the bay. Number 14 Make a splash at Manila Ocean Park's Aqua Adventure. For even more fun, check out the Aqua Adventure inside Manila Ocean Park. The perfect spot to cool off and add a splash of excitement to your visit. This area features interactive water attractions where kids and adults can play, slide, and enjoy water sprays and fountains. It's a great break after exploring the oceanarium, watching the sea lion and bird shows, and visiting the animal exhibits. Bring extra clothes, get ready to get wet, and make the most of your day with this fun-filled water playground right by the bay. Number 15 Experience nature up close at the Philippine Biodome. Another must-see near Manila Ocean Park is the Philippine Bioddome, a modern attraction that lets you experience nature and wildlife up close without leaving the city. Step inside this lush dome-shaped sanctuary filled with native plants, birds, and other local animals living in a carefully designed habitat. Wander along pathways surrounded by greenery. Spot colorful birds and learn about the Philippines rich biodiversity. As evening comes, don't miss the beautiful light show that transforms the bioddome into a magical glowing space at night. It's a relaxing and educational stop for families and kids to enjoy nature, take photos, and appreciate local wildlife right in the heart of Manila.
